TextENGNew: ``Now then, Kei-chan, no need to be shy.@k@vS14/06/210600449. Go ahead and eat to your heart's content.``
``W-wait a second, hey...!``
Paying no mind to my bewilderment, parfaits, cakes, and other desserts kept arriving one after another...
An endless stream of colorfully decorated desserts of all varieties were being placed on the table before me at a dizzying pace.
As a result, the air around me was already saturated with a cloyingly sweet aroma, enough on its own to make me feel full.

Even the customers at other tables were staring at this spectacle with looks of sheer disbelief. ...I mean, I was overwhelmed enough as it was, so I wished they would stop giving me those curious looks.

``So, which one will you start with?@k@vS14/06/210600451. Every single one is a masterpiece from our patissier~!@k@vS14/06/210600452. By the way, my personal recommendations are this one, this one, and also this, this, and this.``
If you're going to recommend something, at least narrow it down to one! ...No, that's beside the point.
``Anyway, explain this to me.@k@vS14/01/210101304. Setting aside why you suddenly called me out here, what's the deal with this mountain of cakes?``
``Huh? Since you came all this way, I thought I'd treat you to dinner.``
``Is cake the staple food in your household instead of bread, you Marie Antoinette?!``
And with this much food, just how many calories are we talking about? Is she trying to give me metabolic syndrome and shorten my lifespan?
...It started right after I got home from school.
My parents had to rush off to Tokyo for work, and just as I was checking our supply of instant noodles to figure out what to do for dinner -- I got a phone call from Shion.
She said she had 'something to talk about' and summoned me to Angel Mort. And that's how I ended up here...
``So how did this turn into a cake tasting session?``
``What's wrong with that, Kei-chan?@k@vS14/06/210600455. You had fun with Sis yesterday, so now it's my turn to have some f-u-n with you. ...Right?``
Depending on how you interpreted that, it had an incredibly alluring ring to it... Wait, what am I even thinking?
``...A-anyway, that's not the point.``
I gave a deliberate cough and steered the conversation to the real reason I came. Otherwise, there'd been no point in making the trip all the way out here.
``Yesterday... how did it go?``
The smile vanished from Shion's face. After excusing herself to a senior waitress, she sat down in the seat across from me.
Looking more carefully, I noticed there were no other customers near our table. Had Shion arranged things so that no one could overhear us?
...Last night, I'd waited the whole time with my phone by my pillow, but Mion never called.
And the next morning -- that is, this morning -- when I arrived at our usual meeting spot... Mion wasn't there. Apparently, she was absent with a fever.
On top of that, Shion, who was supposed to show her face at school, also failed to appear that day... so naturally, our anxiety only grew.
``I just heard.@k@vS14/06/210600460. ...So Sis was out today, huh.``
``I was about to call her, actually.@k@vS14/01/210101310. ...But then you called me first.``
``Haah, honestly...``
Shion swept her hair back with an exasperated look, then grabbed a glass of juice to calm herself down and chugged it without bothering with the straw.
Then, with a loud thud, she set the glass back on the table and turned to face me across the mountain of cakes.
``...I'll cut straight to the point.``
Shion leaned forward on her elbows with a stern expression. ...Intimidated by her intensity, I instinctively swallowed and braced myself.
``The problem --@k@vS14/06/210600464. has been completely resolved.``
I blinked in confusion, unable to process what she meant, and let out a dumbfounded sound. Seeing my reaction, Shion flashed a mischievous grin before bursting out laughing.
``Aahahahaha~!@k@vS14/06/210600466. I mean exactly what I said.@k@vS14/06/210600467. I know you must be thinking 'You had me worried sick~!' but too bad for you, I'm afraid.``
Shion laughed cheerfully. ...Was it just my imagination, or was her usual cynical edge completely gone? She had a bright, unburdened smile, as if something possessing her had been lifted.
changeReason: New translation completely replaces narration about Mion/Shion reconciling with a description of Shion's changed demeanor; the original asks whether they reconciled, while the new version describes her smile instead.

TextENGNew: ``...Seriously, if that's the case, you should've just told me yesterday instead of being so dramatic!@k@vS14/01/210101313. Shion, you're terrible! And Mion's no better!@k@vS14/01/210101314. I've been worried sick this whole time, you know!``
``Aahahaha!@k@vS14/06/210600469. Well, that goes both ways, doesn't it?@k@vS14/06/210600470. ...Or am I wrong, Maebara Keiichi-san, you two-timer who's been out gallivanting with Rika-chama?``
I had no words to counter Shion's sharp jab. ...She had a point; I'd been spending a lot of time with Rika-chan lately, so I couldn't blame her for seeing it that way.
Even so... I still couldn't tell her the reason why...
``Well, I'll hear your excuse eventually.@k@vS14/06/210600472. ...I actually told Sis to keep quiet -- told her to let Kei-chan stew a little longer.``
``...What's that supposed to mean?``
``If a girl handles everything too smoothly, guys tend to think they don't need to pay her any attention.``
``If she wants to hold your interest, she needs to keep you on edge enough that you don't have time to think about other women.@k@vS14/06/210600475. Just kidding~, teehee.``
``...That's not cute at all, and it's also a massive misunderstanding.@k@vS14/01/210101318. Anyway, get back to the main topic.``
``All right, all right.@k@vS14/06/210600477. ...Well, saying it was between Sis and me isn't quite accurate.``
``To be precise, it was between me and the Sonozaki family.@k@vS14/06/210600479. Sis and I have always gotten along normally. I never actually hated her, you know.``
``...Right.@k@vS14/01/210101320. I always assumed your bickering was just because you were so close... but after what I saw yesterday, I wasn't sure anymore.``

``Is that really how you saw it, Kei-chan?@k@vS14/06/210600481. But you know, the opposite of 'love' isn't 'hate.'@k@vS14/06/210600482. If I didn't care about someone, I'd simply be 'indifferent' -- especially someone like me.``
changeReason: Changes 'like' to 'love' and restructures meaning; the JPN 好き here is being discussed philosophically and the new version shifts the framing from generic 'like/hate' to deeper 'love/hate,' altering the emotional register.

TextENGNew: Shion smiled as she said that. ...Maybe it was just my imagination, but her smile lacked its usual cynical slant. It was a radiant expression, as if something possessing her had been lifted.
``That said, yesterday really was a close call.@k@vS14/06/210600484. ...If Sis hadn't come to talk to me, I think we'd still be on bad terms right now.``
``So Mion did come talk to you?``
``...Yeah. We talked about a lot of things.@k@vS14/06/210600486. Though at first -- and I'm only telling you this, Kei-chan -- I was so frustrated I had absolutely no intention of listening.``
And then Shion told me about what happened yesterday...
The moment I got home, I hurled the nearest pillow at the wall. The dull thump it made as it bounced to the floor only infuriated me more, so I rushed over and kicked it as hard as I could.
I was just thoroughly, utterly miserable.
It wasn't just Mion. Keiichi was right there beside her, and instead of calling her out on her cowardly dithering, he'd shooed me away like I was in the way.
After everything we did to support them... what kind of attitude is that?!
And their first date was at the library? ...Ha, what a pair of children! How carefree do you have to be to enjoy sitting side by side and studying together at this age?
God, it made me furious. When it really mattered, Mion couldn't steel herself, couldn't do a single thing. What was even good about her?
It had always been that way.

Always trying to put on a good face for everyone, never saying what she really thought even though she could just come out and say it -- what a pathetic excuse for a person. And the Sonozaki family and the people of Hinamizawa just prop her up on a pedestal. How blissfully naive, the lot of them.
Infuriating. Sickening. Maddening. Irritating. Hateful. ...Frustrating.
I know... I know. These feelings churning inside my chest aren't really directed at Mion or Keiichi.

If anything, they're not directed at anyone at all. ...And because of that, there's no outlet. I want to let it all out, but it just keeps building up, and it's agonizing, and heartbreaking, and sad... It hurts so much.
This emotion swelling and festering inside me... it's jealousy.
changeReason: Changes 'envy' to 'jealousy'; these are distinct emotions -- envy is wanting what someone has, jealousy implies possessive fear of loss. The JPN 嫉妬 more accurately maps to 'jealousy.'

TextENGNew: Why does Mion get to have such a wonderful boyfriend?
Why does everyone shower Mion with blessings?
changeReason: Old translates as 'golden child' (implying special status), new as 'shower with blessings' (祝福される); the new version more accurately reflects the JPN about being blessed/celebrated by everyone.

TextENGNew: When the person I loved... is gone.
changeReason: Changes 'the boy I like' to 'the person I loved'; shifts from present to past tense, from casual 'like' to deeper 'loved,' and from 'boy' to gender-neutral 'person,' altering the emotional weight.

TextENGNew: When nobody ever welcomed me at all.
Tears spilled endlessly, overflowing.
I should have been able to endure it. I thought if I just kept it locked away deep in my heart, it would eventually fade.

But... but I just couldn't. Not after seeing the two of them enjoying themselves like that. Not after seeing how he protected her like that... It was unbearable. I hated it. There was nothing I could do about it.
``Satoshi-kun... Satoshi-kun... uuu, uuuwaaahh...!``
Tears fell one by one onto my bedsheets, leaving stain after stain.
Satoshi-kun... why did you have to disappear?
If you were still here, I'm sure I could have celebrated Mion's happiness from the bottom of my heart.
Even if Kei-chan came between us, I could have brushed it off with a teasing 'Oh my, things are getting steamy~' and not batted an eye.
I want to see you. I want to hear your voice. I want to talk with you. I want to gaze at that shy, bashful face of yours forever and ever and ever.
And I want you to tell me I did well. ...Tell me I was good for keeping my promise. Tell me I've been looking after Satoko properly all this time.
...You don't need to thank me. I've been having a wonderful time with Satoko, too.
I found so many, so many wonderful things about her that I never knew before, and I think she's grown fond of me too.
Besides, the reason she can eat pumpkin now? That's all thanks to me.
changeReason: Changes 'squash' to 'pumpkin'; the JPN カボチャ specifically refers to kabocha, and 'pumpkin' is the more standard translation of this specific Japanese ingredient.

TextENGNew: So... please praise me... I want you to pat my head, like you used to...!
``Uuu... aaahh... uwaaahhh...``
Just then, the doorbell rang. When I lifted my tear-soaked face, the bell rang again, as if in pursuit.
I glanced at the clock. ...It was already quite late. Who on earth would come at this hour?
``......Shion, it's me...``
I was a little surprised. ...It wasn't unusual for someone I knew to drop by at this hour, but Mion had never once come this late before.

Especially after a fight like ours... Normally, she'd put some distance between us, wait for things to cool down, then call up acting like nothing happened, and we'd let it fizzle out. That was our usual pattern.
I grabbed a tissue from my bedside to wipe my tears and stood up. Then, deliberately putting on an air of displeasure to disguise my nasal voice, I spoke.
``What do you want at this hour, Sis?@k@vS14/06/210600494. Isn't this rather rude?``
``...Sorry...@k@vS14/03/210300713. I just... wanted to talk to you, Shion...``
``I'm afraid I have nothing to say to you.@k@vS14/06/210600496. Please go home.@k@vS14/06/210600497. If you don't hurry back, Granny will surely give you an earful.``
``...That's fine, no matter what Granny says...``

``Look, even if you say that, it's a problem for me.@k@vS14/06/210600499. Knowing Granny, she'll think I'm the one keeping you here.@k@vS14/06/210600500. And then I'll end up being the villain, won't I?``
``...Yeah, you're probably right.@k@vS14/03/210300716. So you don't have to open the door.@k@vS14/03/210300717. I'll talk from out here... just please... listen...``
I let out an exasperated sigh. How stupid and naive can this so-called family head be...?
``Talk if you want, be my guest.@k@vS14/06/210600502. But I want to take a shower.@k@vS14/06/210600503. If that's fine with you, say whatever you like and then go home.``
``...Thank you, Shion...``
From beyond the door, I could hear sobs mixed in with her voice. ...Well then, time for my shower. Let me grab a change of clothes and a towel.
``...All this time, I thought I did everything I could for Satoshi,@k@vS14/03/210300720. that I gave it my best.``
``I asked Granny and the Sonozakis about his whereabouts. And when Ooishi started suspecting Satoshi,@k@vS14/03/210300722. ...I begged Dad to step in and keep him from being caught.``
``So when I found out Satoshi had disappeared and there was no way to learn where he'd gone,@k@vS14/03/210300724. ...honestly, I just convinced myself there was nothing more I could do...``
...Yes, I know all that already. Did you really come all this way just to say something so obvious? How utterly ridiculous.

``It was the same when I learned about you and Satoshi, Shion.@k@vS14/03/210300726. I tried my hardest to convince Granny and everyone in the family that Satoshi was a good kid, that they should accept your relationship.``
``...But nobody would agree...@k@vS14/03/210300728. It was so frustrating, so sad... I always wanted to apologize to you and Satoshi...``
What is this... a confession? Does she honestly think that's enough to earn my forgiveness...?
``Sorry... I'm sorry... I'm so sorry...!``
God, how annoying... Maybe I should just make her leave.
With that thought, I ran my fingers through my hair and walked toward the door --
``What you really wanted from me...@k@vS14/03/210300731. wasn't that at all...!``
Mion's voice from beyond the door.
It was so choked with tears and sobs... I could barely make out the words.

``When I... fell in love with Kei-chan... that's when I finally understood...@k@vS14/03/210300733. If everyone opposed a relationship between Kei-chan and me... I'd do everything in my power to persuade them, until every last one of them accepted it...``
changeReason: 'Started to like' becomes 'fell in love with'; significant emotional escalation matching the intensity of JPN 好きになって in the context of a love confession.

TextENGNew: ``Not just with effort -- with desperation, ready to throw away my life...!@k@vS14/03/210300735. And if someone still refused, I'd go so far as to kill them...!``
......What is this girl saying?
changeReason: 'What is this lunacy?' becomes 'What is this girl saying?'; the old version characterizes the speech as insane, while the new version focuses on incomprehension. JPN 馬鹿か means 'Is she an idiot?'

TextENGNew: ``Even if he disappeared!@k@vS14/03/210300737. I'd tear the whole village apart searching, and if that wasn't enough, I'd move on to the next town, and the next, and keep searching, and searching, and searching!``
``If I searched all of Japan and still couldn't find him, I'd go abroad!@k@vS14/03/210300739. If anyone claimed they'd seen him, I'd chase that lead even if it was only a one percent chance!``

``...And if I still couldn't find him, I'd suspect everyone around me was lying, and I'd confront them, interrogate them, demand answers!@k@vS14/03/210300741. I'd do anything -- anything to see Kei-chan again!!``
``...And if someone killed Kei-chan, I'd kill them with my own hands...!!@k@vS14/03/210300743. Those feelings, those kinds of feelings...!``
``...Shion, all this time... for an entire year, you've been...@k@vS14/03/210300745. And I never even noticed... I was truly... truly the worst...!!``
``The power of the Sonozakis! The power of the Sonozaki family head!!@k@vS14/03/210300747. There had to be so much more I could have done with that power...!!!``
``Shut up!!@|@y@k@vS14/06/210600506. Shut up, shut up, shuuuut uuuuuuup!!!``
I couldn't hold it in any longer. I hurled every dark feeling I'd been suppressing all this time at my idiot sister on the other side of that door.
The feelings I'd planned to keep buried forever -- my true, scalding yet frigid, pitch-black emotions -- could no longer be contained.
``It doesn't matter what you say now or what excuses you make!!``
``Satoshi-kun is gone -- he's gone because of all of you!!``
``Apologizing after everything's over and done with won't bring Satoshi-kun back!!!``
``I'm sorry... Shion, I'm truly sorry...!@k@vS14/03/210300749. What I should have done wasn't just help you and Satoshi...!``
``It was to protect your happiness, no matter what it took, no matter what I had to sacrifice...!!``

``...You knew all along... you understood all along, didn't you?!@k@vS14/06/210600511. And yet you did nothing!@k@vS14/06/210600512. You're Mion... even though you're Mion, you did nothing for me and Satoshi-kun!!``
changeReason: Old 'You do know... You did know' vs new 'You knew all along... you understood all along'; the new version adds emphasis on the sustained duration of knowledge.

TextENGNew: ``...Gh... ugh... uwah... Sorry, I'm sorry... Shion...!!``
``...That's enough...!@k@vS14/06/210600514. I don't want to hear your hollow apology at this point.``
``...Are you satisfied now?@k@vS14/06/210600516. Then just go home already... please...!``
``......Shion... listen...@k@vS14/03/210300753. I... like Kei-chan.@k@vS14/03/210300754. I love him so much...!!``
``I told you to go hooooome!!``
``But!!@k@vS14/03/210300756. ...I won't go out with Kei-chan... until Satoshi is found.``
``I'll stay just friends with him... I won't go out alone with him like today ever again!!@k@vS14/03/210300758. Even if he gets close with someone else, I'll just watch in silence!!``
``...Huh?@k@vS14/06/210600519. What are you even saying...?@k@vS14/06/210600520. I'm not going to play along with your self-satisfac--``
``That's why!!@k@vS14/03/210300760. Let's search together... for Satoshi!!``
What is this idiot saying...?
``...Sis, I don't understand what you mean...?@k@vS14/06/210600523. Why would you do something so absurd just to find Satoshi-kun...?``
``Because I'm... an idiot!!@k@vS14/03/210300762. A hopeless, total idiot!!!``

``I want to search for Satoshi too... I want to find out where he went!@k@vS14/03/210300764. But unless I do this, I'll never truly understand how desperate you feel!!@k@vS14/03/210300765. Because I... am an idiot!!``
``Unless I carry the same pain as you, Shion, I know I'll just end up compromising again!!``
At that moment, Mion placed her hand over mine.
...Her fingers.
On her left hand, the nails of her little finger, ring finger, and middle finger were misshapen -- just like mine.
changeReason: Old mentions 'three fingernails looked as ugly as mine' while new specifies 'little finger, ring finger, and middle finger were misshapen'; the new version includes the specific fingers (小指から中指の三本) from the JPN.

TextENGNew: ``What... happened to your hand...?``
changeReason: Old 'What's this...?' vs new 'What... happened to your hand...?'; significantly different questions -- the new version directly asks about the injury, matching JPN これは……どうしたの.

TextENGNew: Tears spilled from Mion's eyes.
I didn't need to ask. The same wounds as mine. ...The same atonement.
Even the way they were healing looked exactly like mine.
...So she'd inflicted the same wounds on herself, around the same time as me...?
``......It wasn't fair... that only you had to have your nails torn off... hic... It was too cruel... hic...!``
``Please... please, Shion...!@k@vS14/03/210300769. I know it's too late... I know you have every right to say that...!@k@vS14/03/210300770. But let me search with you...!``
``I love Kei-chan just as much as you love Satoshi...!@k@vS14/03/210300772. But I'll keep these feelings locked away... until we find Satoshi...!@k@vS14/03/210300773. I promise... I promiiiise!!``
changeReason: 'I like Kei-chan' becomes 'I love Kei-chan'; 好き in this emotional confession context is closer to 'love,' changing the intensity of Mion's declaration.

TextENGNew: Idiot... what an idiot... This girl... my one and only... twin sister... is such a hopeless... utterly hopeless... colossal fool...!!
What are you saying?
What are you apologizing for?
``Uuu... uuuwaaahh... aaahh...!!``
Back then, Mion truly, genuinely poured her whole heart into fighting for my sake.
Even when I had my nails ripped off as punishment... even though it had nothing to do with her, she endured it right alongside me.
And now... she wanted to share my pain with me again...?
Think carefully.
Remember... properly this time.
The one who failed... wasn't it actually me?
I'd depended on Mion, unwilling to act on my own, just resenting and hating everything around me... nothing more than throwing a tantrum...!!
``...You're disturbing the neighbors...@k@vS14/06/210600532. Hurry up and come inside...``
I caught my breath when I saw her crouching at my feet.
She had been... here on this cold ground... prostrating herself, even though no one was here to see it...?
The tears wouldn't stop again. ...But somehow, the heavy, dark sludge that had been smoldering in my chest moments ago... vanished, as if purified, and my whole body felt genuinely light.
``It's late, so... just stay the night.@k@vS14/06/210600535. ...Let's get scolded by Granny together, shall we...?``
Without knowing who reached out first... we held each other. And we cried, and cried, and sobbed our hearts out. ...To anyone watching, we must have looked like absolute fools.
Even so.
I thought to myself that I loved this girl, hopelessly and completely.
``...So, well, that's basically what happened.@k@vS14/06/210600537. Last night, we slept in the same bed together for the first time in ages.``
``I see... That's good to hear.``
Hearing her story brought me both relief and a deep sense of fulfillment.

``And since we had the chance... we were actually together until just a little while ago.@k@vS14/06/210600539. We both skipped school and spent the day chatting about nothing in particular.@k@vS14/06/210600540. Ahahahaha...!``
Shion laughed playfully as she said that. ...And yet, her cheerful smile, as if some burden had been lifted, told me their time together had been anything but meaningless.

``So, well... as a self-proclaimed future sister-in-law, I thought I'd share a bit of happiness with my future brother-in-law.@k@vS14/06/210600542. Although, if you're trying to match that happiness in desserts from this place, even this amount barely scratches the surface~.``
``...I'm not confident I can even finish this 'barely scratching the surface' amount.``
Still, I could more than understand how happy Shion must have felt. And if that was the case, rising to the occasion was what a real man would do!
``All right, I'm the man they call K of Angel Mort!@k@vS14/01/210101325. This much food is nothing on an empty stomach!``
``Ahahaha!@k@vS14/06/210600544. I knew you'd say that, Kei-chan!@k@vS14/06/210600545. Go on, eat up, eat up!@k@vS14/06/210600546. I'll gladly take additional orders anytime~!``
``...No, that I'll have to pass on.``
``By the way, Shion.@k@vS14/01/210101328. ...That scrapbook you got from Takano-san the other day -- what kind of thing was it?``
I asked about something that suddenly came to mind while wolfing down my fourth parfait.
At the time, Mion had intervened before I could see much more than the cover, but I recalled it being filled with all sorts of notes and newspaper clippings.
...Meanwhile, Shion seemed to have had her fill of sweets and was now savoring a coffee. How unfair.
``She didn't give it to me, you know?@k@vS14/06/210600548. She just lent it to me for a bit.``
With that, Shion said 'Excuse me for a moment' and headed to the cloakroom, returning shortly after. In her hands was that very same scrapbook.

``When I mentioned I was looking into Hinamizawa's 'demoning away' incidents, she offered to lend it to me in case it might help.@k@vS14/06/210600550. I did flip through it a bit, but it was so steeped in the occult that I gave up pretty quickly.``
Shion handed me the scrapbook and I looked through it. ...Sure enough, there were dubious clippings about aliens and supernatural phenomena scattered throughout.
I noticed something that caught my eye a little past the middle and stopped turning pages. The article discussed the 'demoning away' phenomenon in Hinamizawa.
``Did you read this part too, Shion?``
``Huh? Yeah, Sis and I read that together.@k@vS14/06/210600552. Sis just laughed and said it was nothing but a pack of lies.``
``...What do you think, Shion?@k@vS14/01/210101332. About the demoning away.``
``Huh...?@k@vS14/06/210600554. Wait, Kei-chan, don't tell me you actually believe this stuff?``
``This is basically a gossip piece -- a mishmash of speculation and pure fiction, you know~?``
Shion laughed heartily after saying that. Just like Mion, she didn't seem to put any stock in the scrapbook's contents whatsoever.

``...Well, I might have taken it more seriously if I'd read it back when I was suspicious of the Sonozakis.@k@vS14/06/210600557. But after hearing all those stories from Sis yesterday and today, I realized something.``
``...That the curse of the 'demoning away' actually has no consistency to it.``
``No consistency...?``

``Right. The reasons the victims are 'demoned away' have been getting increasingly vague.@k@vS14/06/210600560. If the curse is supposed to target people who defied village rules regarding the dam project... then what about the construction worker who killed the site foreman and vanished four years ago?``
``Setting aside the couple three years ago, what about the priest's wife two years ago?@k@vS14/06/210600562. And then last year -- the housewife, and... Satoshi-kun?``
``When you think about it calmly, it quickly becomes clear that these people weren't exactly defying the village.@k@vS14/06/210600564. And on top of that --``
``On top of that?``
I realized I'd been parroting her words back like an echo, but I pressed on anyway. Shion gave a mildly exasperated shrug and shook her head before answering.

``...The Sonozaki family actually hasn't done a thing.@k@vS14/06/210600566. They didn't cause the curse, and they didn't try to prevent it either.@k@vS14/06/210600567. ...When it came to being afraid of the rumors, they were no different from the rest of us.``
``...It's just as Shii-chan says.@k@vS14/05/210500718. Oryou and the Sonozaki family were never the ones behind the major incidents.``
Rika-chan confirmed this, corroborating what Shion had said.
...Apparently, while people suspected Oryou Sonozaki of masterminding the kidnapping of Minister Inukai's grandson during the dam conflict five years ago, she hadn't actually been involved at all.
However, since the opposition movement was at its peak and the Sonozaki family's massive campaign was in full swing, she took advantage of the suspicion people naturally directed her way.
``So the Sonozaki family in Hinamizawa is...``
``In the truest sense, merely a symbol.@k@vS14/05/210500720. And the same holds true for the other two of the Three Great Families -- the Kimiyoshis and the Furudes.``

``However, because absolute authority was needed to unite the village, the Three Families created an object of worship called 'Oyashiro-sama' and treated the related incidents as the work of a 'curse.'``
``That fear and misinterpretation spread among the people, and thus the legend of 'demoning away' was born.``
``...That sounds like a real nuisance, honestly.``
In response to my honest impression, Rika-chan said 'Mii' with a wry smile and shrugged.

``It couldn't be helped. The construction site foreman's case could be explained as an argument gone wrong since he was unpopular with his workers.@k@vS14/05/210500724. But the accident involving the dam-supporting Houjou couple was far too symbolic...``
``At first, even I suspected the Sonozaki family might be pulling strings behind the scenes.``
``But... the Sonozakis didn't do anything.@k@vS14/01/210101338. You figured that out too, right, Rika-chan?``
Rika-chan silently gave a small nod. Seeing her like that, I hesitated over whether to ask, but... I steeled myself and spoke up.
``Rika-chan, about...@k@vS14/01/210101340. Do you know why your parents died...?``
``...Mii. What would you do with that information?``

``Just a theory of mine.@k@vS14/01/210101340_341_02. Shion said there was no consistency among the victims of the 'demoning away' curse.@k@vS14/01/210101340_341_03. ...I think that's because the motives behind each case are all different.``
The site foreman four years ago -- workplace friction between a subordinate and his boss.
The Houjou couple three years ago -- an accident... or retribution for supporting the dam project against the village's will.
The housewife one year ago -- a thrill-seeker piggybacking on the curse.
Regardless of the truth, the motives behind the killings were completely inconsistent.
The only common thread was that the victims could be labeled 'enemies of the village.' And even that was a stretch for the housewife -- she was essentially insignificant to the village.
...Which only made the incident two years ago involving Rika-chan's parents stand out as fundamentally different.
While there may have been a reason they were targeted, every aspect -- the motive, the circumstances -- was so unnatural and baffling that it was impossible to even begin imagining the culprit.

``The incident two years ago is the only one where no human motive can be found.@k@vS14/01/210101342. If it wasn't simply death from illness, then the curse is the only explanation.@k@vS14/01/210101343. And that's precisely what led everyone to believe so strongly that the curse was real.``
``...In other words, that incident was the catalyst that transformed all the previous incidents into a single mysterious phenomenon.``
In other words, you could say that the very moment the 'curse of Hinamizawa' was born was precisely then.
Rika-chan still hadn't told me whether the person who killed her parents was Miyo Takano, but... if that were the case, then the heart of this conspiracy seemed to lie right there.
``...That's remarkable, Keiichi.``
Rika-chan let out an impressed sigh and broke into a bright smile.
``I see... so you've arrived at that conclusion.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1017. I knew you could do it, Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1018. Even I had never thought of it from that angle.``
Rika-chan let out a breath and fell into contemplative silence. Then, gazing up at the starry night sky, she spoke in a quiet, emotionally guarded voice.
``......Death from illness.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1020. Even if I told you that, you wouldn't believe me, would you?``
``But as I'm sure you suspect... it certainly wasn't the work of a curse.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1022. And it's perfectly natural that you couldn't discern a human motive behind it.``
``Because the culprit used the 'curse' as a smokescreen.``
``Used the 'curse'...?@k@vS14/01/210101349. Then the culprit really is...``
``...Mii. Please, let's leave it at that for now.``
Then, Rika-chan lowered her head apologetically.
I wanted to dig deeper, but... trusting that she would tell me someday, I held my tongue.
``More importantly, Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500741. I'd like to see that scrapbook from Takano.``
``Sure. ...Here.``
I opened my bag and pulled out Takano-san's scrapbook, which I'd borrowed from Shion.
Rika-chan took it without ceremony and began flipping through it... and her expression gradually darkened.
``So she was showing this... to Shii-chan, to Rena, and to you, Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500743. At last... now I understand.``
``You understand... what exactly?``
``This scrapbook.@k@vS14/05/210500745. This was the trap all along.``
changeReason: Old 'These scrapbooks' (plural) vs new 'This scrapbook' (singular); JPN このスクラップ帳 is singular. The number matters as it's a specific plot-relevant item.

TextENGNew: ``...Uh, what do you mean?``
``A delusion remains a delusion as long as it stays inside your head. But the moment any reasoning -- no matter how contrived -- is committed to writing, it becomes a theory.``

``And when you see material written by someone else, it evokes a sense of solidarity: 'It's not just me.' And when it appears in magazines and similar media, it gains credibility as something 'plausible.'``

``So if someone already harboring suspicions about the Sonozaki family or Hinamizawa were to read this, they'd become convinced their thoughts were more than mere delusions, and their distrust would deepen further.``
``...So people only see the reality they want to see, is that it?``
I remembered reading something to that effect in a magazine somewhere, a long time ago.

``...Shii-chan was able to escape this trap because she reconciled with Mii-chan.@k@vS14/05/210500750. It was a close call.@k@vS14/05/210500751. If things had continued down that path, I'm sure Shii-chan would have...``
``...Rika-chan?``
``You really saved us, Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500753. I'm so glad -- truly glad -- to have you as one of our friends.``
Rika-chan smiled brightly as she said that. ...I didn't quite understand, but it seemed like I'd been of some help to her.
``Still, though...``
``What is it, Keiichi?``
``Shion said Takano-san is the one who put this scrapbook together.@k@vS14/01/210101357. ...If that's the case, wouldn't the person who made it believe in its contents even more strongly?``
``As if she wants to witness the curse with her own eyes...``

It was just an offhand remark, but the moment Rika-chan heard it, her eyes went wide. As if struck by a sudden flash of insight, she turned back to the scrapbook and began rapidly flipping through the pages.
Then, the moment she opened to a certain page, Rika-chan gasped and froze as if turned to stone.
``Wh-what's wrong?@k@vS14/01/210101360. Rika-chan?!``

``Yes.@k@vS14/09/210900031. I will kill you.@k@vS14/09/210900032. The queen carrier's corpse is the key to this operation.@k@vS14/09/210900033. You will become a pawn in an ugly political power struggle, far away in Tokyo.@k@vS14/09/210900034. And with that, the legend of Onigafuchi Village will come to its end.``
``...No way... Takano intends to artificially trigger the curse and kill every single villager...?!``
Rika-chan's words left me utterly stunned.
Behind the conspiracy to murder Rika-chan...
lay something far worse.
The total annihilation of Hinamizawa.
